mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or the predictive coefficient flips positive in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.0 percent trigger immediate equity deleveraging and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, systematically driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal from GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in quarterly panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply constraints that erode corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in post-shock recovery periods do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in regime-filtered samples.
